name: NSW Construction and Infrastructure Scheme
description: Rules for construction, maintenance, and infrastructure procurements under NSW Government policy frameworks. Includes Building Code, Security of Payment, and Local Industry Participation obligations.
source: NSW Construction Procurement Policy; Building and Construction Industry Security of Payment Act 1999; Local Industry Participation Policy

pre_checks.title: Check eCentral and agency construction panels
pre_checks.body: Check whether your agency has pre-qualified construction contractors or standing panels via eCentral (ecentral.nsw.gov.au) before proceeding to open market. Whole-of-government construction panels may apply depending on the value and type of work.

pre_checks.title: Engage your agency's property and construction team
pre_checks.body: Construction procurement has specialist requirements. Engage your agency's property, facilities, or construction management team early. Independent cost estimation is recommended for works over $250,000.

obligations.title: Security of Payment obligations apply
obligations.body: The Building and Construction Industry Security of Payment Act 1999 applies to all construction contracts. Ensure contracts comply with progress payment provisions and payment schedules. Payment terms must not exceed 20 business days for subcontractors.
obligations.policy: Building and Construction Industry Security of Payment Act 1999

obligations.title: Local Industry Participation Plan required
obligations.body: Construction procurements above $10 million must include a Local Industry Participation Plan. Procurements above $1 million should document how local industry has been considered in the procurement approach.

obligations.title: Work Health and Safety requirements
obligations.body: All construction contracts must include WHS obligations consistent with NSW Work Health and Safety Act 2011 and SafeWork NSW guidelines. For major works, a Construction Safety Management Plan should be required.
obligations.policy: Work Health and Safety Act 2011; SafeWork NSW Guidelines

obligations.title: Licensing and insurance verification
obligations.body: Verify that all contractors hold appropriate contractor licences under the Home Building Act 1989 (where applicable) and hold required public liability and professional indemnity insurance before contract execution.
obligations.policy: Home Building Act 1989; NSW Fair Trading

obligations.title: Independent cost estimate recommended
obligations.body: For construction works above $250,000, obtain an independent quantity surveyor or cost estimate to establish a budget benchmark before going to market. This supports value for money assessment and tender evaluation.

pathway_overrides.if_arrangement_exists: Use agency construction panel or eCentral arrangement
pathway_overrides.if_no_arrangement: Proceed with recommended pathway — note construction-specific steps below

steps: Check eCentral and agency construction panels for applicable pre-qualified suppliers
Obtain independent cost estimate if works exceed $250,000
Confirm WHS, licensing, and insurance requirements are included in specification

approval_additions.note: Agency property or construction management team approval required before going to market
